title: viola kohalana
intro: Viola kohalana, also known as the Hawaiian violet, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Viola kohalana is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
